claim: Glow lowers a neural network dataflow graph into a two-phase strongly-typed IR: a high-level node graph for domain-specific optimizations and a low-level instruction-based address-only IR for memory optimizations (instruction scheduling, static memory allocation, copy elimination); a lowering phase reduces high-level operators to a small set of linear-algebra primitives so new hardware backends need not implement every operator
